cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out. The question is about what we can learn from the article regarding plastic bottles. The options are A through D. First, I need to recall the information in the article. The article mentions several points about plastic bottles. Let me go through the relevant parts again. The article says that "some 2.7 million tons of plastic are used worldwide to bottle water each year. The plastic can take between 400 and 1,000 years to break down." So option A says they break down in less than 300 years, but the article states 400-1000, so A is incorrect. Next, the article talks about energy use. It says that if 10% recycled materials were used, they would save a lot of gas. This implies that making plastic bottles isn't cheap in terms of energy, so option D, which claims they are cheap to make, is probably wrong. Option B suggests they can be used for many other things. The article doesn't mention anything about other uses of plastic bottles. It focuses on the environmental impact and safety issues. So B might not be the right answer. Option C states they are a risk to the environment.
